raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS - A white napkin is partially visible on a patterned tablecloth. The napkin is a flat, flexible object. STRATEGY - The task is to pull the napkin out. This requires grasping one end of the napkin and pulling it away from the tablecloth. Failure modes include not grasping the napkin properly or pulling too hard and tearing it. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the napkin at its edge for a stable grasp; Step 2 grasp the edge of the napkin; Step 3 pull the napkin away from the tablecloth.**
Goal: any_in(napkin, hand); stable(napkin)
Scene: napkin, tablecloth
Step 1: reach(napkin)
  affordance_region: the edge of the napkin
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, napkin, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, napkin) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(napkin)
  affordance_region: the edge of the napkin
  contact: holding(napkin) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(napkin)
Step 3: pull(napkin)
  affordance_region: an empty area away from the tablecloth
  contact: surface_contact(napkin, tablecloth, =, 0.05) [completion]; released(napkin) [completion]
  pose: stable(napkin) [completion]
  done_when: surface_contact(napkin, tablecloth, =, 0.05) AND released(napkin)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: an empty area away from the tablecloth
